questions.question: Why is per-subscription state central in pub/sub design?
options: Because subscribers consume at different speeds and need independent progress and replay behavior.
Because topics do not store events durably.
Because pub/sub systems never support filtering.
Because all subscribers must acknowledge in the same order.
questions.explanation: Subscribers are intentionally independent from each other.

questions.question: What question most changes a pub/sub storage design?
options: How long independent replay windows need to be for subscribers.
What font the operator console should use.
Whether publishers run on Linux or macOS laptops.
Whether subscribers prefer camelCase or snake_case fields.
questions.explanation: Replay and retention drive cost and control-plane shape.

questions.question: Which metric most directly shows that one subscriber is becoming a storage problem for the platform?
options: Retained bytes and backlog age attributed to that subscription.
Average event payload indentation depth.
The number of topics created this quarter.
Median TLS certificate age on publisher hosts.
questions.explanation: Lagging subscribers often appear first as retention pressure.

questions.question: Why is server-side filtering a trade-off rather than an automatic good?
options: It reduces subscriber work but adds platform CPU cost and operational complexity.
It guarantees perfect topic ordering.
It removes the need for replay controls.
It makes slow subscribers impossible.
questions.explanation: Filtering moves work and complexity into the platform.

questions.question: What is the cleanest way to protect live delivery from one subscriber's large replay?
options: Isolate replay lanes or budgets from normal live-delivery capacity.
Disable acknowledgements during replay.
Delete the subscription's cursor.
Force all subscribers to replay whenever one subscriber does.
questions.explanation: Replay and live traffic need separate controls.

questions.question: Which failure is most directly addressed by per-subscription backlog budgets?
options: A slow subscriber forcing unbounded retention growth.
A publisher sending malformed JSON once.
A dashboard query running slowly.
A timer wheel drifting by milliseconds.
questions.explanation: Budgets keep one subscriber from consuming unlimited shared storage.

questions.question: Why is topic durability not the same thing as subscription delivery success?
options: Because stored events still need per-subscriber progress, retries, and delivery tracking.
Because durable topics prevent subscribers from lagging.
Because subscriptions do not use offsets or cursors.
Because durability only matters for filtered topics.
questions.explanation: Durability and downstream delivery are separate concerns.

questions.question: What design change best helps a shared pub/sub system support mixed subscriber priorities?
options: Separate delivery tiers or reserved capacity for high-priority subscriptions.
A single retry queue for all subscriptions combined.
Removing seek and pause controls.
Disabling filters globally.
questions.explanation: Priority lanes are a direct way to preserve quality for important consumers.
